title: Save pages with sections collapsed
excerpt: We have many pages built out with program outlines, procedure flows, etc. Some of the pages are quite long. Is there a way to save a page with the headings already collapsed so when users visit the page an overwhelming a…

posts.author: Dmcgillic1 (Danielle Lawrence)
posts.cookedHtml: We have many pages built out with program outlines, procedure flows, etc. Some of the pages are quite long. Is there a way to save a page with the headings already collapsed so when users visit the page an overwhelming amount of information isn’t easily displayed? It also makes it difficult to navigate to the section that you need, as the anchors in the top left are pretty small.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: When you say ‘page’, do you mean a board or a monday Doc?

posts.author: reyans428g (Reyans Guar)
posts.cookedHtml: There are effective ways to make long pages easier to navigate by collapsing content under headings by default. Here’s a breakdown of your options depending on what you’re using to build your site (e.g., HTML, CMS like Confluence, Notion, SharePoint, or a custom web app).

posts.author: Dmcgillic1 (Danielle Lawrence)
posts.cookedHtml:
Reyans Guar:

Here’s a breakdown of your options depending on what you’re using to build your site (e.g., HTML, CMS like Confluence, Notion, SharePoint, or a custom web app).

Hello! You say “Here’s a breakdown of your options depending on what you’re using to build your site (e.g., HTML, CMS like Confluence, Notion, SharePoint, or a custom web app).”, but then there is no additional information. Can you send a breakdown of the options? Thank you for your help!
